tables.title: Post-Cardiac Arrest Care Targets
rows: TTM for 5 days: 32–34°C × 48h then 36–37.5°C, or 36–37.5°C throughout
Titrate FiO2; avoid hyperoxia (worsens reperfusion injury) and hypoxia
Use age-based formula: 70 + (2 × age in years) for children 1–10 years
Ensures coronary perfusion pressure during resuscitation
Both hypo- and hyperglycemia worsen neurologic outcomes
Includes initial cooling phase (if used) plus normothermic maintenance
Multimodal: exam, EEG, MRI, biomarkers, evoked potentials

tables.title: Systolic Blood Pressure Thresholds for Children
TTM Protocol Comparison
rows: Active fever prevention (antipyretics, cooling as needed)
May provide additional neuroprotection in selected cases
Fewer complications (no shivering, coagulopathy, or infection risk from hypothermia)
May be considered in selected cases
Strict temperature control for full 5 days
Strict fever prevention — temperature must never exceed 37.5°C
